OpenICE source code is based on real medical device data types defined in AAMI ICE standards (ASTM 2761-09, AAMI 2700-1)

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is an open-source reference implementation of medical device standards. The DDS data types (Numeric, SampleArray, AlarmLimit, InfusionObjective, DeviceIdentity, Patient, InfusionStatus, DeviceConnectivity) are verified to exist in the official ice.idl file at https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp. Key verified fields include: unique_device_identifier, metric_id, vendor_metric_id, instance_id, unit_id, value, values, device_time, presentation_time, frequency, manufacturer, model, and serial_number. All expected FHIR R4 resources (Observation, Device, Patient, MedicationAdministration, DeviceMetric, RiskAssessment, MedicationRequest, DeviceUseStatement) are confirmed as valid in the HL7 FHIR R4 specification. However, two data types claimed in metadata do not exist: GlobalSimulationObjective (similar to GlobalAlarmLimitObjective) and LocalModeObjective (similar to LocalAlarmLimitObjective, VentModeObjective). Additionally, the field 'millisecondsPerSample' does not appear in any OpenICE data type definition.
